caption:
This island nation has the best roadtrip in all of Europe 🇪🇺😍

✨Hi! We’re Palak and Vaibhav, a couple based in eastern USA who loves to travel and enjoy the beautiful outdoors. Follow along our journey if you like to hike, go on roadtrips and travel the world with your partner ♥️

🇮🇸 Iceland is one of the most unique and otherworldly countries on the planet. Its stunning scenery coupled with a rugged coastline and colorful landscapes is something you have to experience at least once in your life 🥹🌋

📌 Save this roadtrip which takes you to the best spots that Iceland has to offer 🫶

🛬 Reykjavik- The capital of Iceland and gateway for all international travellers
🟠 The Golden Circle- A beautiful area with spots like Thingvellir, Geysir and Gullfoss
🏕️ Landmannalaugar- Part of the stunning Icelandic Highlands
💦 Seljalandsfos- Stunning waterfall that you can walk behind
💦 Skogafoss- Powerful waterfall with a curtain like appearance 
◼️ Reynisfjar- Beautiful black sand beach with rock formations 
🗼 Vik- Small icelandic town with lush landscapes 
🧊 Glacier Lagoon- Lagoon full of icebergs where you can kayak
💦 Svartifoss- Gorgeous waterfall with Basalt columns 
🌳 Fjaðrárgljúfur Canyon- Stunning canyon with grey rocks and lush scenery
🌳 Mulagljufur Canyon- Green and lush, this canyon has great scenery and waterfalls
⚫️ Vestrahorn- Black sand beach with picture perfect mountains
💦 Hengifoss- Gorgeous waterfall with colorful rocks 
🛘 Studlagil- Canyon made of basalt columns
♨️ Myvatn- Town located near an accessible geo thermal area
🐳 Husavik- Small town famous for world class whale watching
💦 Godafoss- Deep
Blue colored waterfall with lush scenery
⛰️ Kirkjufell- The most picturesque mountain in Iceland
⛪️ Snaefellsnes Peninsula- Home to incredible scenery and Icelandic culture

.
.
.
.
.

#icelandtravel #midnightsun #iceland #roadtrip #europetravels
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
If you are planning to visit Utah and are impacted by the new National Park entry rules, these must visit spots outside the National Parks are the perfect way to experience Utah 🫶😍

✨Hi! We’re Palak and Vaibhav, a couple based in eastern USA who loves to travel and enjoy the beautiful outdoors. Follow along our journey if you like to hike, go on roadtrips and travel the world with your partner ♥️

🪨 Factory Butte- This otherworldly monolith is 6,300 ft or 1,920 m high and you can drive very close to it with a bit of off roading
🚘 Potash Road- Stunning drive going through a beautiful red rock canyon with massive red rock wall on one side and the Colorado River on the other
🌈 Bentonite Hills- A stunning display of colors makes this spot really special. It is nearby NASA’s Mars Desert Research Station in Hanksville
🌘 Moon Overlook- Also called Moonscape Overlook or Skyline Rim Overlook, this spot will really make you think you’re on the moon
🛻 Marlboro Point- This amazing hidden gem is accessible via a 4x4 if you want to make it all the way to the point. You can opt to park around 0.4 mile away if you are more comfortable with that
🏜️ Goblin Valley State Park- Dense hoodoos define the surreal landscape of this beautiful park. A $20 fee is required for this state park
🗼 Long Dong Silver- A strikingly beautiful Spire in Hanksville, Utah. Requires ~1 mile hike to reach this spot
🐎 Dead Horse Point State Park- One of the best sunrise we have ever seen was inside this park. The warm light of the sunrise illuminates the red rocks with a striking red glow. We highly recommend a visit here

.
.
.
.
.

#utah #utahroadtrip #visitutah #moab #usaroadtrip

caption:

✨ For more such content with bucketlist destinations and epic wildlife, follow @peekinourjournal and get the most amazing ideas for your next trip 🫶

🛻 Safari in Africa is one of the best experiences and it is so worth the hype! The entire continent is blessed with some amazing wildlife so it can be pretty hard to choose where to go 🌍

🦁 With the highest lion population in the world along with other big cats and ton of game, Tanzania has to be the pick of the bunch. The country is blessed with stunning scenery and every safari day brong along something new 🐘🐆

🇹🇿 Tanzania has a lot of National Parks and reserves to choose from. We highly recommend Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire and Arusha 📌💫

caption:
We found the most beautiful sunrise in Utah ☀️😍

✨If you love to travel and want to see more of such content, follow @peekinourjournal to get amazing ideas and guides for your next trip!

Moab, Utah is home to some of the most otherworldly landscapes. With Arches and Canyonlands National Parks taking up all the headlines, this lesser known state park has to be on your Moab list! 🙌🏻

📍 Dead Horse Point State Park offers breathtaking scenery and you can watch the most amazing sunrise here. The scene of the red rocks illuminated with a warm orange glow is just perfection 🥹

🚗 This park is very close to Moab, about a 40 minute drive from the town. You need to pay a $20 entry fee 💲

.
.
.
.
.

#deadhorsepoint #moab #utah #utahroadtrip #visitutah
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
Is there a better summer destination than Iceland? 🥹⛰️☀️

✨If you love to travel and want to see more such content, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your next trip 🫶

Iceland is one of the most beautiful countries on the planet with some really diverse and stunning landscapes everywhere you look! 🇮🇸

🌅 The Summer season is arguably the best time to visit this country. All the spots are accessible during this time and there is a phase where the sun never sets, making the experience even more magical 😍

If you’re planning a trip to Iceland, we highly recommend going in the summer. You can opt for hotels, hostels or BnBs but the best way to experienceit has to be with a campervan 🚐

🥶 The summers in Iceland can also be very cold so be prepared and pack enough layers. We highly recommend spending at least a week exploring the Ring Road 🛣️ 

☃️ If you want to see the Northern Lights, the winter months would be perfect to visit. However the days would be much shorter and a lot of roads may be closed 🚙

.
.
.
.
.

#iceland #icelandroadtrip #icelandtravel #Iceland trip #roadtrip

✨If you love to travel and want to see more of such content, follow @peekinourjournal to get amazing ideas and guides for your next trip!

🐻 Alaska is an year round destination. The summers offer a ton of activities like hiking, backpacking, fly fishing, and world class bear viewing 🥾

If you have to pick one destination in the USA for 2026 we highly recommend Alaska, no matter what time of the year! ♥️

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
This is DC right now 🌸🥹 

✨For more travel content with amazing destinations, roadtrips and wildlife spots, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your dream trip 🫶

Cherry Blossoms are in full bloom, showing off their large, white, puffy petals with pinkish hues. These magnificent flowers look amazing anytime of the day but the sunrise makes them even more special 🥹

Washington DC, the capital of USA is the most popular destination to see these trees. Our favorite spot here was Tidal Basin with hundreds of Yoshino Cherry trees lining up the basin banks along with views of Washington Monument and Jefferson Memorial 🌊🗼

The sunrise here is just gorgeous ! You can see the cherry blossoms being lit up in the golden light and showing off their magical appeal 🧡

.
.
.
.
.

#cherryblossom #washingtondc #usatravel
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
This is the most beautiful Cherry Blossom destination in USA 🌸😍 

✨For more of such content with amazing destinations and travel guides, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your next trip 🫶

Every year during spring, the cherry blossoms fully bloom and bear incredibly gorgeous flowers. These cotton like flowers with hues of pink line up along the streets of Washington DC and completely transform the city 🥹

The entire capital of USA is filled with these trees and here are our favorite spots to see them 😍

🗼 Tidal Basin- The most popular spot in DC for Cherry Blossoms and for good reason! It is absolutely gorgeous but super crowded
⛲️ Enid A Haupt Garden- A stunning garden located in the heart of the city. You can see a ton of colorful Magnolias here
🚴‍♂️ Hains Point/East Potomac Park- The cherry blossoms line up the entire road here. Beautiful spot for outdoor acrivities like picnic, cycling etc
🏛️ United States Capitol- Located at the end of National Mall, it’s a great spot to see some Cherry Blossoms and enjoy views of this beautiful building
🪦 Oak Hill Cemetery- Located in Georgetown near Dumbarton Oaks, this is a beautiful place to see the blossoms. Please be respectful during your visit
👨‍⚖️ Congressional Cemetery- Located on the east side of DC, this site is operated by the national park service and is a great spot to see Okame cherry blossoms. Again, important to be respectful if you visit

.
.
.
.
.

#cherryblossom #washingtondc #usatravel
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
When the Cherry Blossoms are in full bloom, this is how DC looks 🥹 

✨For more travel content and amazing destinations, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your next trip 🫶

The spring is a great time to visit Washington DC as the city is engulfed with white and pink shades of the magical Cherry Blossoms 🌸

These trees do not stay in bloom for long however and there is a 4-7 day window to enjoy them at their peak. If you can, the next couple of days would be best to visit DC ✈️

.
.
.
.
.

#cherryblossom #washingtondc #usatravel
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
Is Patagonia on your travel bucketlist? 🤔

✨For more travel content with amazing destinations, roadtrips and wildlife spots, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your dream trip 🫶

South America is home to some of the most diverse and jaw dropping landscapes in the world! The southern region of this continent, commonly referred to as Patagonia is arguably the best destination on the entire continent 🥹

This region is shared by the nations Chile and Argentina and is characterised by rugged mountains, strong winds, glacial lakes and abundant wildlife! 🐧 

.
.
.
.
.

Torres Del Paine, Mount Fitz Roy, Los Glaciares, Perito Moreno Glacier, National Park, Mirador Base Las Torres, Mirador de los tres, El Chalten, El Calafate, Puerto Natales, Punta Arenas
ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
These must visit towns in USA will leave you in awe 😍

✨For more travel content with amazing destinations, roadtrips and wildlife spots, follow @peekinourjournal and plan your dream trip 🫶

Some of the best towns in USA are the ones close to nature and these places are some of the best when it comes to this 💯

🏔️ Juneau, Alaska- The gorgeous capital of Alaska is one of the most underrated destinations. More popular as a cruise stop, this place boasts Glaciers, Waterfalls, Mountains, Forests, Wildlife and a cute downtown
🧗 Moab, Utah- A popular town amongst outdoor enthusiasts, Moab has some of the most unique and otherworldly landscapes. Catch the sunrise at Mesa Arch in Canyonlands NP and the sunset in Arches National Park
🦬 Jackson (Jackson Hole), Wyoming- Stunning resort town which serves as the gateway to Grand Teton National Park. It is a popular ski destination and is perfect for all types of travellers. The cowboy culture here is so fun and gives this place a unique identity
🏜️ Sedona, Arizona- This is one of the most scenic towns in the USA. Anywhere you look, you’ll get amazing views of rugged and beautiful red rocks all around you! Its perfect for hiking and other outdoor activities but also has some amazing shopping and fine dining

.
.
.
.
.

#usatravel #usaroadtrip #alaska #utah #bucketlisttravel

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️ one of the best meals I had in Italy 👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

Candlelit stone arches, quiet music, warm bread, sea air drifting in from the Amalfi Coast, and every table around looked like people were having one of those nights they’ll remember forever

That was my experience with this place.

My favorite dish was the Spaghetti al Pomodoro Re Fiascone 🍅 made with an ancient tomato from the Amalfi Coast.

This was genuinely the best pasta I’ve had in my life. 100/10.

The sauce was rich, silky, deeply flavorful, and fresh. Simple ingredients but done so perfectly it almost made me emotional

The tiramisu was unreal too.

The bread service was incredible and every single detail felt thoughtful. The ceramics, the plating, the pacing of the meal, the service, the monastery setting overlooking the sea… elegant without feeling stiff or pretentious.

This is…

📍Ristorante Dei Cappuccini at Anantara Convento di Amalfi, Amalfi, Italy

@anantaraconventodiamalfi 

📌 Save this for your Amalfi Coast trip

The octagonal bell tower rises right out of Cinque Terre’s only natural harbor and you can see it from almost every angle in town.

If you’re walking around Vernazza, don’t just take photos of the harbor but go in here + it’s FREE to enter.

This is…

📍Church of St. Margaret of Antioch, Vernazza, Cinque Terre, Italy 🇮🇹

caption:
‼️2,000 years later people are still coming here for the exact same reason

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

Long before luxury wellness retreats, Roman emperors escaped to this tiny island for the sea air, sunlight, silence, and dramatic cliffs above the Mediterranean.

the smell of lemons everywhere
warm salty air blowing through narrow alleyways
hidden gardens above the sea

This is…

📍 Capri, Italy

🚤 How to get here
Take a ferry or private transfer from Naples, Sorrento, or the Amalfi Coast. Ferries run frequently but mornings are usually calmer.

What to do
Visit the Blue Grotto
Take the chairlift to Monte Solaro
See the Faraglioni rocks by boat
Walk through Anacapri for a quieter side of the island
Visit Villa Jovis, where Emperor Tiberius once lived

What to eat
Lemon granita
Caprese salad (it originated here)
Fresh seafood pasta
Torta Caprese, the island’s famous chocolate almond cake

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️and the gardens here inspired one of the most famous composers in history 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This medieval villa was once one of the largest and most expensive estates on the entire Amalfi Coast.

It was built in the 1200s by the wealthy Rufolo family, and was designed to show power, status, and wealth at a time when Ravello was an important trading town connected to the Mediterranean world.

Today, parts of the villa are still in ruins. You walk through Moorish arches, stone towers, hidden courtyards, and gardens hanging above the sea.

This is…

📍 Villa Rufolo, Ravello, Amalfi Coast, Italy

🚗 How to get here
The easiest way is by car or private driver from Amalfi, Positano, or Salerno. Ravello sits high above the coastline so expect winding mountain roads with incredible views the entire way.

🎟️ Things to know
The villa is famous for its cliffside gardens and terrace overlooking the Amalfi Coast. If you visit in summer, Ravello festival concerts are sometimes held here

📌 Save this for your Italy trip

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️ and one side of this town feels completely different from the other 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This colorful town on the Italian Riviera was once a separate island before nature slowly connected it back to mainland Italy over time.

That natural formation created two completely different bays on each side of town.

One is called the Bay of Silence with calm water, tiny fishing boats, pastel reflections, and peaceful atmosphere.

The other side is lively, social, and full of boats, aperitivo spots, restaurants, etc.

This is…

📍 Sestri Levante, Italy

🚗 How to get here
Sestri Levante is easily accessible by train from Genoa, Milan, Pisa, or Florence and the historic center is walking distance from the station.

🍝 What to eat
Try fresh pesto pasta, Ligurian focaccia, seafood linguine, and sunset aperitivo by the water.

📌 Save this for your Italy trip

Which side would you choose…the peaceful Bay of Silence or the livelier bay?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️location👇🏻 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

Walking through this place honestly felt overstimulating in the best possible way.

Your eyes don’t know where to focus first because every wall, ceiling, archway, and courtyard is layered with insane detail. Arabic calligraphy flows across the walls, reflections through the water, and sunlight changes the colors of the stone

What makes it even crazier is that much of this was built in the 1200s by the Nasrid dynasty during Moorish rule in Spain…nearly 800 years ago.

and despite wars, conquests, and centuries of history, it still feels impossibly ahead of its time.

This is…

📍 Alhambra, Granada, Spain

🎟️ Tips before visiting
Book your tickets weeks in advance because they regularly sell out, especially for the Nasrid Palaces.

Don’t miss
The Court of the Lions, the intricate ceilings inside the Nasrid Palaces, and the Generalife gardens with flowing water channels and views over Granada.

📌 Save this for your Spain trip

Have you ever seen architecture this detailed before?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
A lot of people think Cinque Terre and Amalfi Coast are basically the same trip…

they are NOT even close!

One is slow, colorful, cozy, and easy and the other is dramatic, luxurious, and cinematic.

And I think choosing the wrong one for your travel style and in the wrong season is exactly why some people leave disappointed.

I have broken down all the categories like landscapes, transportation, costs, architecture, and more to help you decide between the two if you can only visit one.

I personally loved both for completely different reasons.

Also…I strongly suggest visiting the Amalfi Coast in winter. It was one of the best Italy experiences I’ve ever had. There were no crowds or traffic, and the entire coastline felt surreal (I had it all to myself).

There were no crowds in Cinque Terre either which made exploring a breeze.

So now I’m curious…

which one are you choosing?

📌 Save this before planning your Italy trip.

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻

This tiny medieval village completely pulled me out of reality for a few hours.

The cobblestones polished by centuries of footsteps, flower boxes were spilling out of wooden windows, and it felt like I was in a storybook.

You can hear birds echoing through the alleyways, forks clinking against plates from hidden restaurants, and the smell of melted butter and fresh galettes drifting through the streets.

This is…

📍 Pérouges, France

🚗 How to get here
Pérouges is about 40 minutes from Lyon by car. You can also take a train to Meximieux and grab a short taxi ride to the village.

What to eat
The famous galette au sucre is a must here. It’s a crispy buttery sugar tart that basically perfumes the entire village.

📌 Save this for your France trip

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️ nobody was rushing here…not even a little 😭

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This is what happens when a country values conversations, friendships, long dinners, and enjoying life more than constantly being busy

kids playing freely in the square while older locals sit in the sun talking for hours, friends gathering outside before dinner, nobody staring at their phones, and the entire atmosphere feels lighter and full of life because of it

for a moment, you stop thinking about notifications, deadlines, and productivity…

This is Italy 🤍

One of my favorite things to do here is honestly just sit in a piazza and observe daily life -
order an espresso
listen to the conversations around you
watch the golden light hit the buildings
and slow down enough to enjoy absolutely nothing for once

📌 Save this for the day you finally book that Italy trip

Do you fully live in the moment or living a constantly busy life on your devices while surrounded by people?

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

Most people visit this place for the Renaissance streets, sunset views, and famous piazza from Twilight saga’s New Moon

but underneath the town, there is an entire hidden underground world that most people have no idea exists

This is the Underground City inside Ercolani Cellars where ancient tunnels, hidden chambers, passageways, wells, and historic spaces stretch beneath the streets.

Some parts date back centuries and it genuinely feels like discovering a secret

Honestly, this ended up being one of the coolest experiences I had in Tuscany

This is…

📍 Ercolani Cellars Underground City
Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy

🚗 How to get here
Montepulciano is best visited by car as part of a Tuscany road trip through Val d’Orcia. Ercolani Cellars is located directly inside the historic center.

What to do
walk through underground tunnels and chambers beneath Montepulciano while learning about the hidden history underneath the town. There are ancient artifacts, Etruscan elements, historic wells, and cellar spaces throughout the experience. 

⚠️ There are free guided tours with wine tasting

📌 Save this for your Tuscany trip

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️The Tuscany road trip you will think about forever👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and road trips around the world @tripuntraveled

This is the route I would actually do if you want medieval towers, hilltop towns, wine villages, cypress roads, Renaissance streets, and countryside views.

📍Volterra
Start here if you want Tuscany with an ancient, mysterious feeling. This town has Etruscan roots, a Roman theatre, medieval streets, and alabaster shops that make it feel completely different

📍San Gimignano
This is the famous medieval Manhattan of Tuscany, with 14 surviving towers still rising above the town. Go early or later in the day because this one gets busy, but the skyline is absolutely worth seeing once in your life

📍Certaldo Alto
This is one of the most underrated stops on this route. It is small, brick colored, quiet, and tied to Giovanni Boccaccio, the author of The Decameron. Take the funicular up

📍Montalcino
Come here for Brunello wine and fortress views. This is a good stop for wine tasting or a long countryside meal.

📍San Quirico d’Orcia
Beautiful and unreal close to the famous cypress trees, Chapel of Madonna di Vitaleta, Horti Leonini, and some of the most iconic Val d’Orcia landscapes.

📍Pienza
End here for the soft, golden, romantic version of Tuscany. Pienza was redesigned as an ideal Renaissance town, and will make you fall in love with Tuscany.

🚗 How to plan this route
Do not try to squeeze all of this into one rushed day unless you enjoy suffering beautifully.

Day 1 - Volterra, San Gimignano, Certaldo
Day 2 - Montalcino, San Quirico d’Orcia, Pienza

But if you have more time, make it 3 days and stay overnight in the countryside.

📌 Save this for your Tuscany trip because this route gives you the best mix of medieval towns, wine country, cypress views, and more.

Ask any questions👇🏻 

__

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️Venice - overrated or unforgettable?👇🏻

🤍 Follow for unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

I finally understand why people either LOVE Venice or absolutely hate it.

But I have to say, those who hate it might be doing this -
they arrive midday
fight crowds around Rialto and San Marco
eat at tourist restaurants
leave a few hours later
and then wonder what the hype was about 🤔

But the biggest Venice hack is to STAY OVERNIGHT

Where to stay?
The San Marco / Grand Canal area for classic luxury Venice and iconic views
Cannaregio for quieter canals and local feel
Dorsoduro for artsy, romantic Venice

Most underrated experience?
Taking a private taxi down the Grand Canal at sunset

For a special experience?
Book one nice aperitivo or dinner with canal views instead of standing in lines. Atmosphere matters in Venice

Beautiful stays worth looking into
@stregisvenice
@amanvenice
@thegrittipalace
@belmondhotelcipriani

What surprised me the most?
Venice doesn’t feel impressive because of one landmark. It feels impressive because an entire city lives on water.

Then there are tiny bookstores, hidden courtyards, quiet canals, faded buildings, laundry hanging above the water…it feels more like a film set than a city sometimes.

📍 Venice, Italy

📌 Save this for your Venice trip

So…Overrated or unforgettable?

©️@tripuntraveled
ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️hidden underground world in the last clip 🤯 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This Tuscan hill town is peaceful with Renaissance buildings, golden light, and lovely cobblestone streets

but underneath it is a maze of underground tunnels, ancient cellars, hidden chambers, and passageways carved beneath the town centuries ago (will share in part 2)

and if this place feels strangely familiar, the streets and piazza here were used in Twilight saga (New Moon) vampire world. They even built a massive fake fountain in the square just for filming.

This is…

📍 Montepulciano, Tuscany, Italy

🚗 How to get here
The easiest way is by car as part of a Tuscany road trip through Val d’Orcia. You can also take a train to Montepulciano Stazione, but the historic center is high on a hill so you’ll still need a bus, taxi, or climb the stairs up 😅

What to do
Explore Piazza Grande and the steep streets, visit the underground tunnels and historic cellars, watch sunset over the Tuscan countryside, and visit the Temple of San Biagio just outside the walls

📌 Save this for your Tuscany trip

Were you able to recognize this place?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️how is it that narrow? 🤯 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

In this tiny Umbrian town, there is an alley called Vicolo Baciadonne, which translates to kiss the women.

It’s only about 53 cm wide, so if two people walk through it at the same time, there is barely enough room to pass.

And they say that is exactly how this alley got its romantic reputation…if you met someone inside, you might have to get very, VERY close 💋

But there’s a lot more to this town…

It’s a red brick hilltop town near the border of Tuscany and Umbria, and a complete hidden gem

There are quiet medieval streets, warm terracotta buildings, Renaissance art, beautiful countryside views, and saffron history that goes back centuries.

This is…

📍 Città della Pieve, Umbria, Italy

🚗 How to get here
The easiest way is by car, especially if you are road tripping through Tuscany or Umbria. It is a beautiful stop between places like Montepulciano, Pienza, Orvieto, and Lake Trasimeno.

What to see
Vicolo Baciadonne
The red brick historic center
The Cathedral of Saints Gervasio and Protasio
Perugino’s Renaissance artworks
Palazzo della Corgna
The countryside views from town

What to try
Look for local saffron dishes, handmade pasta, Umbrian wine, and anything with truffle if it is on the menu.

📌 Save this for your Tuscany or Umbria road trip

Who are you walking this alley with? 👀

caption:
‼️ location and details

🤍 Follow @tripuntraveled for more unique towns and hidden places around the world

This place is only 40 minutes from Girona and 1.5 hours from Barcelona, making it a perfect stop on a Catalonia or Costa Brava road trip.

The dramatic bridge you see here is Pont Vell, and parts of it date back to the 11th century and was actually designed as a defensive trap.

Visiting the town and walking the bridge is completely free.

Things to do here -

Walk across Pont Vell at sunrise or sunset for the most magical views of the town
Explore the tiny medieval streets and stone houses in the old town
Visit the 12th century Jewish ritual bath (Miḳvé), one of the only surviving ones in Spain
Stop by Plaça de la Llibertat for coffee or tapas
Walk along the river for one of the best views of the iconic bridge

This is…

📍 Besalú, Catalonia, Spain

Save it for your Spain trip.

caption:
‼️location👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled 

This place is famous for the coast, but the real charm is watching ordinary life happen in a place that feels anything but ordinary.

This is…

📍 Amalfi, Amalfi Coast, Italy

🚗 How to get here
You can visit Amalfi by ferry, bus, private transfer, or car. The ferry is one of the prettiest ways to arrive, especially from Positano, Salerno, or Sorrento during the season. Driving is beautiful in off season with easy parking.

🚶🏻‍♀️ What to do
Walk the waterfront and get into the side streets. Visit the Amalfi Cathedral, explore without rushing, stop for coffee, explore the little shops, and watch how life moves through the town.

🍋 What to try
Lemon everything. Amalfi is known for its giant lemons, so try lemon cake, lemon granita, limoncello, lemon pasta, or anything with lemon cream.

📌 Save this for your Amalfi Coast trip because the best part of Amalfi is the life happening behind the postcard view everyone shows.

Did you know deliveries still move through some Italian towns like this?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This place in Italy was well known for producing velvets, brocades, damasks, and handmade silk fabrics.

They have made silk velvet for kings, royal palaces, the White House, J.F. Kennedy, and even sacred vestments for the Pope.

Its smooth velvet was even historically supplied to the White House for furnishing the Oval Room.

This is…

📍 Zoagli, Liguria, Italy

How to get here
Zoagli is very easy to reach by train. It’s on the Genoa-La Spezia line or you can drive the scenic SS1 Via Aurelia coastal road.

Things to do
see the Canevaro Castle
walk the seaside promenade along the rocks
visit the small beach below the train station
explore the town
see the old coastal towers
and enjoy the views without the crowds.
There is also a bronze statue of the Madonna of the Sea nine meters deep

📌 Save this for your Italy trip or you’ll likely miss it
